title: Paper and wood machine operatives
description: Create or reproduce handmade objects for sale and exhibition using a variety of techniques, such as welding, weaving, pottery, and needlecraft. Directly supervise and coordinate the activities of mechanics, installers, and repairers. May also advise customers on recommended services. Excludes team or work leaders. Repair percussion, stringed, reed, or wind instruments. May specialize in one area, such as piano tuning. (Based on 41 related US occupations.)

Judging the Qualities of Objects, Services, or People
Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards

Communicating with People Outside the Organization
Performing for or Working Directly with the Public

tasks.task: Inspect and examine raw materials, containers, products, and finished workpieces for defects and conformity to specifications, using tools such as calipers, gauges, or visual inspections.
Remove completed or defective products or materials, placing them in specified areas or on moving equipment, such as conveyors.
Install, adjust, and maintain woodworking machines and equipment, including changing worn parts and lubricating machines.
Set up, program, and operate woodworking machines such as saws, drill presses, and CNC machines, ensuring proper adjustments and alignment for accurate cutting, shaping, or assembly.
Feed, move, and sort woodstock and products through machines or conveyors using appropriate tools and equipment.
Trim and shape wood parts according to specifications, using tools such as saws, planes, and chisels, while ensuring precise measurements.
Record production data, including dimensions, quantities, and equipment performance issues, using specified forms and monitors.
Monitor machine operations to detect malfunctions and ensure conformance to specifications, making necessary adjustments.
Prepare and package finished products for shipment, using appropriate packing materials, and labeling packages.
Create or reproduce handmade objects for sale, ensuring quality throughout the production process.
Perform general cleaning and maintenance of work areas, machines, and tools to ensure safe and efficient operations.
Inspect and maintain equipment for leaks, malfunctions, and repairs; clean, oil, and lubricate machines; and track operational data such as temperatures and pressures.
Load and unload equipment chambers, transport finished products, and manage the operation of various woodworking machines to shape and process wood materials.
Measure and mix chemical ingredients for finishing processes, and apply finishing ingredients such as paint, stain, lacquer, or varnish to wood surfaces and furniture.
Set up, operate, and adjust machinery for wood processing according to specifications, including trimming, sanding, and shaping products to meet design requirements.
Construct, assemble, and repair wooden articles, including furniture and fixtures, ensuring adherence to specifications and quality standards.
Draw blueprints or specifications for the production of patterns and models, and select appropriate materials for construction and finishing.
Organize materials and inventory for production processes and ensure accurate records of supplies, patterns, and customer specifications are maintained.
Examine and assess furniture for damage or deterioration, and determine required repair methods, including refinishing and restoring the original appearance.
Direct and supervise the activities of workers involved in woodworking tasks to ensure proper processes and quality standards are followed.
Maintain safety precautions during woodworking and finishing processes to prevent accidents or damage.
Dump, scoop, or pour prescribed solid, granular, or powdered materials into equipment or containers for further processing or storage.
Implement appropriate industrial emergency response procedures.

Observe and compare colors and consistencies of products to instrument readings and laboratory standard test results; inspect articles for defects and verify qualities such as clarity, cleanliness, consistency, or texture.
Flush or clean equipment using steam hoses or mechanical means; clean and maintain machines and work areas with soaps, solvents, or lubricants.
Make minor repairs, lubricate, and maintain equipment with hand tools; correct machinery malfunctions by performing actions such as removing jams and informing supervisors as necessary.
Drain equipment and pump water or other solutions through to flush and clean tanks or equipment.
Read plant specifications to determine product requirements and modifications of plant procedures; read work orders to communicate machine settings and adjustments, and supply specifications.
Measure, weigh, or mix specified amounts of ingredients or materials for processing or loading into tanks or equipment.
Initialize machinery such as pumps, feeders, conveyors, and presses; start, stop, or adjust machines to process materials according to prescribed specifications.
Monitor equipment operation to detect malfunctions, and report or resolve production problems; observe gauges and controls to ensure equipment runs efficiently.
Collect and inspect random samples or test product characteristics for compliance with specifications; collect samples of materials or products for laboratory analysis.
Transport materials, supplies, and finished products using forklifts, handtrucks, or manual movement between storage and work areas.
Align and stitch or glue materials to join parts; construct, decorate, or repair products using sewing machines, glue, hand tools, and other materials according to specifications.
Clean, polish, and finish materials or products; inspect for defects and perform repairs or modifications as needed.
Record gauge readings, production logs, or test results for monitoring operations; maintain production records such as quantities and specifications.
Adjust machine components, controls, or settings according to specifications; calibrate gauges and meters to ensure proper operation.
Install, maintain, or repair equipment parts and machinery using hand tools and other means; prepare equipment for operation by assembling parts and attachments.
Directly supervise and coordinate activities, including communicating processing instructions to other workers, and monitor their performance.
Dye, polish, paint, or engrave materials to achieve desired aesthetics or utility; apply adhesives to secure materials together.
Cut out parts following patterns using knives, shears, or machine presses; drill or punch holes and secure metal accessories.
Repair and recondition leather products, such as shoes and bags, according to client specifications, using appropriate tools and techniques.
Monitor environmental factors impacting equipment performance, such as humidity and temperature, and make necessary adjustments.
Program computers or electronic instruments, such as numerically controlled machine tools, including programming computer numerical control (CNC) machines.
Fit and assemble parts to make or repair machine tools, including changing worn machine accessories, such as cutting tools or brushes, using hand tools.
Confer with engineering, supervisory, or manufacturing personnel to exchange technical information, as well as with numerical control programmers to ensure functionality of new programs or machinery.

Dismantle machines or equipment using hand tools or power tools to examine parts for defects and replace defective parts where needed; diagnose machine tool malfunctions to determine need for adjustments or repairs.
Extract or lift jammed pieces from machines, using fingers, wire hooks, or lift bars.

Design fixtures, tooling, or experimental parts to meet special engineering needs, and study sample parts, blueprints, drawings, or engineering information to determine methods and sequences of operations needed to fabricate products.

Inspect workpieces for defects and measure workpieces to determine accuracy of machine operation using rules, templates, or other measuring instruments; verify accuracy of shapes and sizes of objects.
Start machines and turn handwheels or valves to engage feeding, cooling, and lubricating mechanisms; select the proper coolants and lubricants and start their flow.
Dispose of scrap or waste material in accordance with company policies and environmental regulations while also separating scrap waste and related materials for reuse, recycling, or disposal.
Set up and operate machines, such as lathes, cutters, shears, borers, millers, grinders, presses, drills, or auxiliary machines, to make metallic and plastic workpieces.
Read blueprints or job orders to determine product specifications and tooling instructions, and compute data, such as gear dimensions or machine settings, applying knowledge of shop mathematics.
Perform minor electrical and mechanical repairs and adjustments to machines and notify supervisors when major service is required; maintain machine tools in proper operational condition.

Move controls or mount gears, cams, or templates in machines to set feed rates, cutting speeds, depths, and angles; set machine stops or guides to specified lengths as indicated by scales, rules, or templates.

Observe machine operation to detect workpiece defects or machine malfunctions; adjust machines as necessary, and make adjustments according to product specifications using knowledge of machine operation.
Maintain supplies of tools, equipment, and materials, and order additional supplies as needed; clean work area.

Record operational data such as pressure readings, lengths of strokes, feed rates, or speeds, and verify dimensions of products using measuring instruments, such as micrometers, calipers, and protractors.
Lift and position workpieces manually or with hoists, securing them in hoppers or on machine tables, faceplates, or chucks, using clamps.
Pack and ship pottery to stores or galleries for retail sale, and prepare work for sale or exhibition while maintaining relationships with retail and resource networks for selling artwork.
Operate gas or electric kilns to fire pottery pieces, and mix and apply glazes to pottery pieces, using tools such as spray guns.
Raise and shape clay into wares such as vases and pitchers on revolving wheels, and adjust wheel speeds as clay pieces are formed.
Smooth surfaces of finished pieces using rubber scrapers and wet sponges, and verify depths and dimensions of cuts or carvings to ensure adherence to specifications.
Trim or remove excess material using scrapers, knives, or band saws, while also examining finished ware for defects and measuring dimensions.
Drill holes and cut or carve moldings and grooves in stone according to diagrams and patterns, and carve rough designs freehand or by chipping along marks on stone using mallets and chisels.
Shape, trim, or touch up roughed-out designs with appropriate tools to finish carvings, and lay out designs or dimensions from sketches or blueprints on stone surfaces.
Select chisels, pneumatic or surfacing tools, or sandblasting nozzles, and determine the sequence of use; program CNC machines for precise operations.
Operate and maintain finishing machines to grind, drill, sand, bevel, decorate, wash, or polish glass or glass products, including shaping, bending, or joining sections of glass using hand tools.
Heat glass to pliable stage using gas flames or ovens.
Remove burrs, sharp edges, rust, or scale from workpieces using files, hand grinders, wire brushes, or power tools; smooth surfaces of molds using scraping tools or sandpaper.
Monitor and refill fluid levels in machines, such as oil and coolant; lubricate workpieces and brush lubricating compounds on workpieces.
Operate drying chambers to dry or finish molded ceramic ware.
Specifically design and create glass objects using blowpipes and artisans' hand tools and equipment.
Determine types and quantities of glass required to fabricate products, while also researching artistic objects or graphic materials to plan carving or cutting techniques.
Engrave or stamp identifying symbols, letters, or numbers on products; teach pottery classes as a form of community engagement.

Thread and hand-feed materials through machine cutters or abraders, and manually or with lifting equipment position and secure workpieces on machines using holding devices, measuring instruments, hand tools, and hoists.
Adjust controls to set cutting speeds, feed rates, depths of cuts, and synchronize machine operations, and turn valves to direct the flow of coolant onto work areas.
Maintain and repair machines by cleaning, lubricating, and replacing worn parts using hand tools, as well as repairing, maintaining, and replacing parts on dies.
Start machines and observe operations to produce workpieces, detect malfunctions, and ensure specifications are met, including monitoring machine operation and recording data.

Read work orders or blueprints to determine setup specifications and operational sequences for machine setup, and study machining instructions to assess dimensional or finish specifications, tooling requirements, and material needs.

Measure and inspect parts to ensure they conform to specifications using measuring instruments such as calipers, micrometers, and gauges, and examine completed workpieces for defects such as chipped edges or marred surfaces.
Select, align, and bolt positioning fixtures, stops, and specified dies in machines, and install and adjust cutting tools, and evaluate the quality of workpieces against specifications.
Create functional or decorative objects by hand using a variety of methods and materials, and apply finishes to crafted objects.
Sketch or draw objects to be crafted, develop concepts or creative ideas for craft objects, and create prototypes or models of the handmade objects.
Confer with customers to assess needs, advise on services, and promote products through advertising and marketing strategies.
Cut, shape, fit, join, mold, or process materials with hand tools, power tools, or machinery to meet product specifications.
Monitor and adjust machine setups to maintain product quality, including troubleshooting issues that arise during production.
Significantly engage with craft trends, customer buying patterns, and develop designs using specialized software tools to inspire craft creations.
Verify that workpiece reference lines are parallel and ensure proper alignment of workpieces on machines, using measuring instruments.
Collaborate with other workers regarding machine setups and operational specifications, ensuring effective communication during the production process.
Plan, execute, and record the operational and maintenance procedures for machine-operated tasks, ensuring adherence to safety standards and operational efficiency.
Execute the sorting and categorization of finished products and defective pieces based on specific criteria, contributing to efficient production flow.
Prepare raw materials for crafting processes, loading them into machines or into the workspace efficiently, ensuring safety and organization throughout the workspace.
Adjust machine settings to achieve desired product outcomes in terms of dimensions and specifications, while ensuring operational consistency during machine operation.
Write simple programs for computer-controlled machine tools, adjusting operations based on observation of the processes and production criteria.
Operate a variety of machines to complete tasks such as milling, turning, and drilling, ensuring conformity to specifications and operational sequences.
Install and adjust various machine components, including dies and attachments, using hand tools and measuring instruments, ensuring accuracy in operations.
Maintain and monitor inventory levels of materials and supplies for crafting to ensure uninterrupted production processes, following organizational protocols.
Research and analyze ongoing trends in crafting and products to adapt designs and marketing strategies, providing valuable insights to enhance marketability.
Disassemble and organize parts for machines and tools during maintenance or repair sessions to streamline operations and facilitate efficient problem-solving.
Examine and inspect raw materials and finished products for compliance with quality standards and specifications before use or shipment to customers.
Gather feedback from customers post-sale to assess satisfaction and to determine areas for product improvement or development.

Implement changes to machine programs and enter new specifications using computers. Transfer commands from servers to CNC modules via computer network links. Set up future jobs while machines are operating. Clean machines, tooling, or parts using solvents or solutions and rags. Stack or load finished items or place items on conveyor systems. Maintain machines and remove or replace broken or worn machine tools using hand tools. Examine objects, systems, or facilities and analyze information to determine needed installations, services, or repairs. Requisition materials and supplies, such as tools, equipment, or replacement parts. Compute estimates and actual costs of factors such as materials, labor, or outside contractors. Interpret specifications, blueprints, or job orders to construct templates and lay out reference points for workers. Conduct or arrange for worker training in safety, repair or maintenance techniques, operational procedures, or equipment use. Perform skilled repair or maintenance operations using equipment such as hand or power tools, hydraulic presses, or welding equipment. Inspect, test, and measure completed work using devices such as hand tools or gauges to verify conformance to standards or repair requirements. Monitor employees' work levels and review work performance. Monitor tool and part inventories and the condition and maintenance of shops to ensure adequate working conditions. Inspect and monitor work areas, examine tools and equipment, and provide employee safety training to prevent, detect, and correct unsafe conditions or violations of procedures and safety rules. Determine schedules, sequences, and assignments for work activities based on work priority, quantity of equipment, and skill of personnel.
